title: Appendix 7 - De-identification techniques and privacy evaluation in synthetic data
purpose: Reference guidance on de-identification, disclosure risks, and evidence-based privacy evaluation for synthetic data projects.
body: De-identification is a collection of technical and organisational approaches intended to reduce the likelihood that data can be associated with an identifiable individual. It is a risk-management exercise rather than a binary state, and stronger privacy protection often reduces utility.
Privacy evaluation in synthetic data should consider identity disclosure, membership disclosure, and attribute disclosure. Residual risk can persist even when direct identifiers are removed.

No single privacy metric is sufficient. A robust assessment uses a portfolio of evidence that may include re-identifiability metrics, similarity and distance-based metrics, adversarial attacks, and empirical auditing.
Similarity-based and average-case metrics can miss serious leakage. Differential Privacy is a property of the generation process, not the output dataset, and should still be validated empirically.
Use this appendix during Step 4 to guide de-identification choices, Re-identification Risk Assessments, privacy auditing, and reporting across multiple synthetic data generation runs.
